name: Loop of the city of Navarrenx
name_original: Boucle de la cité de Navarrenx
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Susmiou are situated in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department, offering diverse terrain from the gentle foothills of the Pyrenees to more challenging mountain passes. The region features dramatic valleys like the Vallée d'Aspe and Vallée d'Ossau, alongside impressive natural formations such as the Gorges d'Holzarté and dense woodlands like the Forêt d'Iraty. This landscape provides a varied backdrop for road cyclists, combining river valleys with significant elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Susmiou The most popular road cycling route is Loop of the city of Navarrenx, a 43.4 miles (69.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area around the historic fortified bastide of Navarrenx. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Préchacq bridge – Navarrenx loop from Navarrenx, a moderate 30.2 miles (48.7 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop starting and ending in Navarrenx. Local road cyclists also love the Point Henri Laclau – Mauléon-Licharre loop from Navarrenx, a 59.4 miles (95.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Susmiou is defined by the foothills of the Pyrenees, expansive valleys, and deep gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Susmiou are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Susmiou's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Susmiou?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes around Susmiou, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes have been explored by more than 1000 cyclists using komoot.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Susmiou?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the gentle foothills of the Pyrenees to more challenging mountain passes. You'll find routes through dramatic valleys like the Vallée d'Aspe and Vallée d'Ossau, impressive natural formations such as the Gorges d'Holzarté, and dense woodlands like the Forêt d'Iraty. This landscape provides a varied backdrop, combining river valleys with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Susmiou offers options for all abilities. There are 15 easy routes and 48 moderate routes available, providing less strenuous rides through the scenic landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10526848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préchacq bridge – Navarrenx loop from Navarrenx&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.2-mile path that offers a scenic loop.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near Susmiou?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 17 difficult routes. The region's proximity to the Pyrenees means you can tackle renowned mountain passes, some of which are famous from the Tour de France. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e853033862/loop-of-the-city-of-navarrenx" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of the city of Navarrenx&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 43.4-mile route with significant elevation gain, exploring the area around the historic fortified bastide.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while road cycling near Susmiou?
A: Many routes pass by historical sites. Just 2km from Susmiou, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2625175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navarrenx&lt;/a&gt;, a historic fortified bastide with impressive 16th-century architecture. Other notable sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sauvelade Abbey&lt;/a&gt; and various charming churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7326229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Église paroissiale Saint-Jean-Baptiste&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7326215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Germain-d'Auxerre Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic features along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the stunning Vallée d'Aspe and Vallée d'Ossau, or experience the dramatic Gorges d'Holzarté, Gorges de Kakoetta, and Gorges d'Ehujarre. The Forêt d'Iraty offers dense woodland scenery, and the Lac de Bious-Artigues provides a tranquil natural site that can be part of a scenic excursion.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Susmiou?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Susmiou are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10527284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastion of Echoes – Navarrenx loop from Navarrenx&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31-mile circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40994406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navarrenx – Pretty fortified village loop from Susmiou&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent moderate option starting directly from Susmiou.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Susmiou?
A: The road cycling routes in Susmiou are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 29 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from gentle foothills to challenging climbs, and the beautiful natural and historical landscapes encountered along the way.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Susmiou or nearby towns?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Susmiou and surrounding towns like Navarrenx, which serve as popular starting points for many cycling routes. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability.

Q: Can I reach Susmiou by public transport with my bike?
A: While direct public transport options specifically catering to bikes might be limited in smaller villages like Susmiou, major towns in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department often have better connections. It's recommended to check regional bus or train services for their bike policies and routes to nearby hubs like Oloron-Sainte-Marie, from where you could cycle to Susmiou.

Q: Are there long-distance cycling routes that pass through the Susmiou region?
A: Yes, the Vélosud, a 650km cycle route that traces the gentle foothills of the Pyrenees, passes through the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department. This route offers an accessible cycling adventure with splendid views of mountain summits and diverse landscapes. More information can be found on &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-velosud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Susmiou?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. Spring offers lush greenery and moderate temperatures, while autumn provides cooler weather and beautiful fall foliage. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, but higher elevations offer relief. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation suitable for cyclists near Susmiou?
A: Yes, towns like Navarrenx and Oloron-Sainte-Marie, which are close to Susmiou and often serve as route starting points, offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Many establishments in these areas are accustomed to catering to outdoor enthusiasts, including cyclists.
tips: Peaceful roads and panorama on the chain of the Pyrenees in good weather. We never regret going there.
After a very difficult climb with passages up to 20%, a beautiful ridge road that leads back to Monein.

Located on the route of the Santiago de Compostela trail, as the statues and shell signs found in the town remind us, Navarrenx is a member of the association of the most beautiful villages in France.

The town has a remarkable heritage. This medieval bastide has the particularity of being one of the first fortified cities in France. It has an austere and military side and presents classical style buildings.
